strengths: Strong SQL and query optimization experience
Hands-on ETL and pipeline building with Python
weaknesses: Limited demonstrated experience connecting BI to BigQuery
Shorter tenure in data roles (approx 2+ years)
No explicit mention of service accounts/DevOps permissions
improvement_bullets: Architected and managed analytics-ready tables using SQL, adopting dbt-like modular transformation patterns to ensure clean, maintainable, and well-documented data models for downstream BI consumption.
Engineered and maintained robust ETL pipelines to sync data from PostgreSQL and Salesforce CRM into a central data warehouse, ensuring seamless data integration and reliability.
Executed comprehensive data validation checks within Python and SQL scripts to maintain high data integrity, reducing error rates in downstream reporting by 30%.
Partnered with cross-functional stakeholders to deliver data storytelling presentations, translating complex technical insights into actionable business strategies for organizational growth.
Configured service accounts and managed security permissions for data warehouse access, ensuring secure and scalable connectivity between our data sources and BI reporting layers.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Analytics Engineer opportunity with Pivotal Health. I am interested in this opportunity because of your commitment to simplifying complex healthcare reimbursement through an integrated, AI-driven platform. What interests me about working for Pivotal Health is your focus on providing clarity and control to providers, which inspires me because I am passionate about building the data infrastructure that turns raw information into meaningful business outcomes. I believe my proficiency in building end-to-end data pipelines and my deep experience in SQL-driven data modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by balancing technical engineering rigor with an obsession for business impact. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in our reporting, which led me to architect an automated data pipeline that processed raw business data into clean, analytics-ready tables. This initiative resulted in a 40% reduction in data processing time and significantly improved the speed at which leadership could make informed decisions—a direct parallel to the work required at Pivotal Health. Ultimately, I know that the Analytics Engineer role at Pivotal Health would allow me to bring my expertise in SQL and data warehouse management to build the scalable infrastructure your team needs. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies (MassMutual) enabled me to develop my robust data integration skills. I collaborated with engineering and product teams to integrate disparate sources into a unified PostgreSQL schema, which streamlined our development processes and improved data accuracy by 30%. I am excited by the opportunity to use my dbt and warehouse management skills in working on the product-to-warehouse pipeline at Pivotal Health. I know that my technical foundation and business-centric mindset align with what it takes to succeed at the Analytics Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
